File: /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/openshift-servicemesh-plugin/source/plugin/src/kiali/components/IstioWizards/RequestRouting/MatchBuilder.tsx

Apply ALL of the following 5 fixes to this file:

### Fix 1
Line: 150
Rule [semver-packages-react-core-src-components-button-button-buttonprops-d-ts-button-variant-type-changed]:
Property 'Button.variant' had its type changed (property): Return type of `variant` changed from `'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'` to `'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'`

File: packages/react-core/src/components/Button/Button.ButtonProps.d.ts
Before: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'
After: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  propName: variant
  propValue: secondary

Fix strategy:
Strategy: PropTypeChange
Component: Button
Prop: variant
From: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'
To: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'

Code context:
```

  150            variant={ButtonVariant.secondary}
  151            isDisabled={!props.isValid}

```

### Fix 2
Line: 97

## InputGroup Family Migration

```jsx

    <InputGroupItem isBox={...} isDisabled={...} isFill={...} isPlain={...} />
    <InputGroupText component={...} isDisabled={...} isPlain={...} />
  </InputGroup>
```

```
(no snippet)
```

### Fix 3
Line: 149
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-button]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Button> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Button> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <Button variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></Button>
  After:  <Button variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <Button> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <Button><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</Button>
  After:  <Button icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</Button>

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: InputGroupItem

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: Button
Prop: icon

Code context:
```
  148        <InputGroupItem>
  149          <Button
  150            variant={ButtonVariant.secondary}

```

### Fix 4
Lines: 65, 108
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-menutoggle]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <MenuToggle> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <MenuToggle> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <MenuToggle variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></MenuToggle>
  After:  <MenuToggle variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <MenuToggle> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <MenuToggle><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</MenuToggle>
  After:  <MenuToggle icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</MenuToggle>

Incident context:
  componentName: MenuToggle
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: Dropdown

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: MenuToggle
Prop: icon

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

Code contexts:
```
  (line 65):
   64            toggle={(toggleRef: React.Ref<MenuToggleElement>) => (
   65              <MenuToggle
   66                ref={toggleRef}

  (line 108):
  107            toggle={(toggleRef: React.Ref<MenuToggleElement>) => (
  108              <MenuToggle
  109                ref={toggleRef}

```

### Fix 5
Line: 8
Rule [sd-test-dropdown-transitive-behavioral-changes]:
Dropdown is affected by behavioral changes in rendered sub-components:
- Dropdown renders Popper which has a behavioral change: Default value for 'appendTo' prop on Popper changed from 'inline' to () => document.body

Tests for Dropdown may need updating. Fix options depend on the specific change:
- For portal changes: use waitFor(), popperProps=, or within(document.body)
- For role/DOM changes: update getByRole() queries to match new roles
- For attribute changes: update querySelector/getAttribute calls

Incident context:
  importedName: Dropdown
  localName: Dropdown
  module: @patternfly/react-core

Code context:
```
    7    InputGroupItem,
    8    Dropdown,
    9    DropdownList,

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/openshift-servicemesh-plugin/source/plugin/src/kiali/components/IstioWizards/RequestRouting/MatchBuilder.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
I'll start by reading the file to understand its current state.

Now let me analyze each fix:

**Fix 1** (Line 150): `Button.variant` type changed — but `ButtonVariant.secondary` is still a valid value in the new type (`'secondary'` exists in both old and new). No code change needed.

**Fix 2** (Lines 97, 140): `TextInput` components at lines 97 and 140 are direct children of `<InputGroup>` without being wrapped in `<InputGroupItem>`. In PF v6, all children of `<InputGroup>` must be wrapped in `<InputGroupItem>` or `<InputGroupText>`. I need to wrap both `<TextInput>` elements.

**Fix 3** (Line 149): Button with icon content — looking at the Button at line 149, it contains only text `{t('Add Match')}`, no icon element. There's no icon to move to the `icon` prop. No code change needed.

**Fix 4** (Lines 65, 108): `MenuToggle` icon content — looking at both `MenuToggle` instances, they contain only text (`{props.category}` at line 71, `{props.operator}` at line 114). No icon elements to move. No code change needed.

**Fix 5** (Line 8): Behavioral change note about Dropdown's Popper `appendTo` default. This is a test-awareness note. The file itself has no tests. No code change needed in this file.

The only real change needed is **Fix 2**: wrap the two `<TextInput>` components in `<InputGroupItem>`.
Let me verify the final file looks correct:
The file looks correct. All `<InputGroup>` children are now properly wrapped in `<InputGroupItem>`. Let me verify there are no companion test files:

No companion test files found. The file is complete and correct.

## Changes Applied

| Fix | Rule | Status | Details |
|-----|------|--------|---------|
| **Fix 1** | `Button.variant` type changed | **No change needed** | `ButtonVariant.secondary` is still a valid value in the new type union — `'secondary'` exists in both old and new type definitions. |
| **Fix 2** | `sd-cf-inputgroup-inputgroup-req-wrap` (InputGroup family migration) | **Applied** | Wrapped the `<TextInput>` at line 97 (header name input) and the `<TextInput>` at line 140 (match value input) each in `<InputGroupItem>`. In PF v6, all direct children of `<InputGroup>` must be wrapped in `<InputGroupItem>` or `<InputGroupText>`. |
| **Fix 3** | `sd-icon-children-to-prop-button` (icon → `icon` prop on Button) | **No change needed** | The `<Button>` at line 149 contains only text children (`{t('Add Match')}`), no icon element from `@patternfly/react-icons`. Nothing to move to the `icon` prop. |
| **Fix 4** | `sd-icon-children-to-prop-menutoggle` (icon → `icon` prop on MenuToggle) | **No change needed** | Both `<MenuToggle>` instances (lines 65 and 108) contain only text children (`{props.category}` and `{props.operator}` respectively), no icon elements. Nothing to move to the `icon` prop. |
| **Fix 5** | `sd-test-dropdown-transitive-behavioral-changes` (Dropdown Popper behavioral change) | **No change needed** | This is a test-awareness advisory about Dropdown's Popper `appendTo` default changing. No companion test files exist for this component. No code change required in the source file. |
